code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    # Helper to format numbers: drop trailing .0 and add commas for thousands
    def _fmt_number(val: str) -> str:
        try:
            num = float(val)
            if num.is_integer():
                num = int(num)
                return f"{num:,}"
            else:
                # keep one decimal if needed
                return f"{num:,}"
        except ValueError:
            return val

    templates = {
        # Airport‑specific predicates – more natural, include subject placeholder
        "cityServed": "serves {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is {obj} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "The runway length of {subj} is {obj}",
        "runwayName": "The runway name of {subj} is {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "The ICAO location identifier of {subj} is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "The location identifier of {subj} is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "The IATA location identifier of {subj} is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "The first runway of {subj} is {obj} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "The first runway of {subj} is {obj} metres long",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "The first runway surface of {subj} is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "The first runway number of {subj} is {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "The runway surface of {subj} is {obj}",
        # Added/adjusted predicates
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction in {obj}",
        # Generic entity predicates
        "leader": "leader is {obj}",
        "capital": "capital is {obj}",
        "language": "language is {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {obj}",
        "currency": "currency is {obj}",
        "owner": "owner is {obj}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {obj}",
        "founder": "was founded by {obj}",
        "postalCode": "postal code is {obj}",
        "areaCode": "area code is {obj}",
        "largestCity": "largest city is {obj}",
        "countySeat": "county seat is {obj}",
        "mayor": "mayor is {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "leader party is {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {obj}",
        "chief": "chief is {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "hub airport is {obj}",
        "regionServed": "serves region {obj}",
        "nativeName": "native name is {obj}",
        # Biological classification predicates
        "class": "class is {obj}",
        "division": "division is {obj}",
        "order": "order is {obj}",
        "species": "species is {obj}",
        "demonym": "demonym is {obj}",
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                # Strip surrounding quotes from objects if present
                raw_obj = t.object.strip('"')
                # Format numbers nicely when appropriate
                obj = _fmt_number(raw_obj)
                # Some templates need the subject placeholder
                if "{subj}" in tmpl:
                    parts.append(tmpl.format(subj=subject, obj=obj))
                else:
                    parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=obj))
            else:
                # Fallback generic phrase
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                obj = t.object.strip('"')
                parts.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
        # Combine parts into a fluent sentence
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {parts[0]}."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
